series_description:
Complaints received by an agency from the
public concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of the
complaint.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item
number 1.1.048, Litigation Files.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence pertaining to the
formulation, planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or
redefinition of the programs, services, or
projects of an agency and the
administrative regulations, policies, and
procedures that govern them.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
item number 1.1.008,
Correspondence - General, should
be used only for correspondence
that is not included in or directly
related to another records series on
the agency's approved schedule.
For example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by item number 1.1.004,
Legislative Appropriation Requests;
a letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by item number 1.1.002,
Audits, etc. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.011, Executive Orders.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records, purchased with state funds or
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_description: Executive level organization charts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
SEE item number 3.2.010, Human
Resources Information System
(HRIS) Reports.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Surveys returned by the external or
internal customers or clients of an agency,
and the statistical data maintained rating
an agency's performance. This series
includes the Survey of Employee
Engagement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
SEE item number 1.1.067, Reports
and Studies (Non-Fiscal), for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered by the
executive team in conjunction with agency
work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Suggestions submitted by agency
personnel and responses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Reviews of the agency's compliance with
federal civil rights laws.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Records are maintained by HHSC
Civil Rights office.

series_description:
Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency's recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions. Some examples of
transitory information, which can be in any
medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard copy,
etc.) are routine messages; telephone
message notifications, internal meeting
notices, routing slips; incoming letters or
memoranda of transmittal that add
nothing of substance to enclosures; and
similar routine information used for
communication, but not for the
documentation, of a specific agency
transaction.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series that
documents the fulfillment of the
statutory obligations of the agency
or the documentation of its
functions. The disposal of transitory
information need not be
documented through destruction
signoffs (1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (1.2.010), but
agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (1.2.014).

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Activity reports compiled by agency
personnel on a daily or other periodic
bases pertaining to workload monitoring,
task completion times, number of public
contacts, and similar activities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number
1.1.064, Agency Performance
Measures Documentation, for
records needed for documentation
of agency performance measures.
These records have a longer
retention period.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years
comments:
SEE ALSO item number 1.1.071,
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Working Files.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Working Files
series_description:
Working files used in the development of
manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
For CPS policy working files, SEE
AIN 53011010, CPS Policy
Development Files. These records
have a longer retention period. SEE
ALSO item number 1.1.070, Agency
Rules, Policies, and Procedures -
Final.

series_description:
One copy of each state publication. A state
publication is defined as information in any
format that is produced by the authority of
or at the total or partial expense of a state
agency or is required to be distributed
under law by the agency, and is publicly
distributed. The term does not include
information the distribution of which is
solely limited to contractors with or
grantees of the agency, staff persons
within the agency or within other
government agencies, or members of the
public under a request made under the
Public Information Act, Government Code,
Chapter 552. This series does not include
publications that meet this definition, but
are subject to a different retention period
in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code 441.101-
441.106). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will retain
a copy of all state publications
submitted to it on a continuing
bases, subject to periodic
evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further retention.
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the Internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
3.1-3.16).

series_description:
Background material, copy (drafts), original
artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats,
campaign materials, etc. This includes all
work performed both inside and outside
the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Publications in any format that are created
and distributed internally by a state agency
and that do not meet definition of a state
publication. This series includes agency
newsletters that are not distributed to the
public.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
For State Publications, SEE item
number 1.3.001, State Publications.

series_description: Background material, copy (drafts), etc.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Data resident on hard drives that make use
of user-specific information transmitted by
the Web server onto the user's computer
so that the information might be available
for later access by itself or other servers.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of Internet cookies
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations (1.2.001)
or in records disposition logs
(1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_description:
A record of the documents visited during
an Internet session that allows users to
access previously visited pages more
quickly or to generate a record of usage of
a state-owned computer.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of Internet history files
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations (1.2.001)
or in records disposition logs
(1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
associated with in-house training of agency
personnel on personnel policies and
procedures and other policies and
procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training records.
SEE item number 5.4.007,
Hazardous Materials Training
Records.

series_description:
Instructional materials developed by an
agency for training entities or individuals it
regulates or serves. Includes instructor
guides, participant workbooks and
handouts, on-the-job training (OTJ)
checklists, computer-based training
curricula, exams, and social work CEU
approved courses.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This series documents state and federal
grant projects participated in or
administered by state agencies. It includes
grant authorization records, which provide
evidence of the award of grants to or by
agencies and grant performance reports
which describe program performance
related to grants received or made by
agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
For financial grant records, SEE AIN
53009280, Grant Records -
Financial. CAUTION: Retention
requirements may vary depending
on the specific funding agency.
Agencies must ensure that records
are retained for the appropriate
retention period. PEI grant records
transferred from HHSC are located
on a network server shared drive,
FY 2012-2015.

series_description:
Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or email address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that establish
standard office procedures for an agency;
for example, agency style manuals,
telephone protocols, mail room
procedures, print shop and photocopy
ordering instructions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service or by private couriers.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes requests for installation, moves,
service, etc.; and service/repair logbooks,
etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
For service agreements or contracts
related to equipment repairs and
service, retain in accordance with
item number 5.1.001, Contracts and
Leases.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Requests for information preliminary to
the procurement of goods or services by
direct purchase or bid.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: If the request for
information leads to request for
proposal or bid, the request for
information documentation should
be retained in accordance with item
number 5.3.007, Bid
Documentation.

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Official agenda and approved minutes of
DFPS council as required by Government
Code, Chapter 551. An Official Agenda is
one that is followed in an open meeting
and is posted in the notice.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission. CAUTION: This records
series and item numbers 1.1.059,
1.1.060, 1.1.061, and 1.1.062 must
be used for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.
legal_citation:
Texas Human
Resources Code,
Section 40.021(d);
Texas Government
Code, Chapter 551

series_title:
Certified Agendas or
Recordings of Closed
Meetings
series_description:
Certified agendas or recordings of closed
meetings of state boards, commissions,
committees, and councils. A Certified
Agenda is one that is followed during the
closed portion of a meeting or in a closed
meeting.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: This records series must
be used for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an actual
meeting.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: This records series must
be used for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance
with Section 2054.095 and 2056.002,
Government Code.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the plans to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Includes applicant qualifications and
evaluation basis for selection or rejection.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: Interns are considered volunteers.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, electronic, audio,
and audiovisual records, except material
shared on a social media site.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years
comments:
NOTE: This series does not include
public relation material posted on
social media sites. For these
records, SEE AIN 53003220, Social
Media Records.

series_description:
Content posted on an agency's social
media accounts. Social media content is
captured by a third-party social media
archiving solution and maintained by the
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Content posted on an
agency's social media account that
falls under another records series in
this schedule with a longer
retention period should be retained
for the longer period. CAUTION:
The State has no control over
retention policies of social media
sites. State records must be
captured and maintained in a
system under the agency's control.
CAUTION: Before deleting or
deactivating a social media account,
contact the agency's RMO.

series_description:
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes. The records may be
maintained with related information
including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes working files, supporting
documentation, and final reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Consultation records, notes, letters,
memos, email, reports, and other
documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 18 months
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEO. If matter
becomes a grievance or the subject
of counseling or litigation, or
employee is subject to disciplinary
action, the records are subject to
retention in the appropriate records
series. SEE 1.1.048, Litigation Files,
3.1.006, Employee Counseling
Records, 3.1.018, Grievance
Records, 3.1.020, Personnel
Corrective Action Documentation,
and 3.1.021, Personnel Disciplinary
Action Documentation.

series_description:
Includes personnel records for consultants
under contract with the agency or
temporary employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
For legal review of contracts and
leases, SEE AIN 53006020, Contract
Legal Review.

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide. This series includes waivers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
SEE related item numbers 3.1.035,
Performance Bonds, and 5.3.007,
Bid Documentation. SEE item
number 5.2.028, Building
Construction Contract and
Inspection Records, for building
construction contracts and item
number 5.1.017, Contract Log, for
contract logs. For legal reviews of
contracts and leases, SEE AIN
53006020, Contract Legal Review.

series_title:
Bid Documentation -
Executed, renewed, or
amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
series_description:
Includes bid requisitions/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, accepted and rejected bids,
and bid tabulation/evaluation. Associated
with a contract executed, renewed, or
amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_title:
Bid Documentation -
Executed, renewed, or
amended on or before
August 31, 2015
series_description:
Includes bid requisitions/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, accepted and rejected bids,
and bid tabulation/evaluation. Associated
with a contract executed, renewed, or
amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Unsuccessful bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in the bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency. This
series includes Legislative Budget Board
(LBB) Performance Measures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
CAUTION: The retention period
overrides any shorter retention
period for a records series in this
schedule if the records series is
needed for documentation of
agency performance measures.
CAUTION: For copies of supporting
documentation submitted to the
Legislative Budget Board, SEE AIN
53009510, Legislative Appropriation
Requests.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed listings
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas state agencies. For Single
Audit Reports, SEE AIN 53004210.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions. Also includes attorney work
files and other files regarding legal
opinions or advice that are not found
under another records series.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard to
pending litigation. SEE item number
1.1.048, Litigation Files.

series_description: Legal review of contracts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit. Includes legal
files created by regional attorneys or legal
support staff in providing representation
to INV, CPS, or APS.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_description: Includes hearing and fair hearing case files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the Attorney
General on an agency's Public Information
Act activities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code, 2009.054(c), including a mediated
settlement agreement, associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated as
a party on the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Government
Code, 2009.054(c);
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS) or other security organizations, such
as the FBI.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Diligent Search documentation prepared
by FINDRS to be sent to requesting
departments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Records documenting search requests and
transfers of search documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Property, equipment, supply verification.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Series includes sale reports and records
related to donation of equipment.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, or similar instruments of
access to agency or state facilities,
equipment, or automated system.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption,
passengers carried and other related
operational information.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
After request is closed, request may
be exported and retained outside of
the open records request system.
legal_citation:
Texas Government
Code, Chapter 552;
Lisson v. UT
Investment
Management; Tex.
Civ. Prac. & Rem.
Code § 16.051

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public

the open records request system.

Code, Chapter 552;
Lisson v. UT
Investment
Management; Tex.
Civ. Prac. & Rem.
Code § 16.051

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records and their
timely, authorized disposition including
documentation describing how a system
operates and necessary for using the

input and output specifications, and
system flow charts; program descriptions
and documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,

document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate
and interpret data in an automated system
such as a data element dictionary, file
layout, code book or table, and other
records that explain the meaning, purpose,
structure, logical relationships, and origin
of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

records, SEE AIN 53001210, Agency

Protective Services (OPS) resulting

Sustained Perpetrator. For OPS

IMPACT codes ACH, AHC, and PAR.
For eligibility records, including
Foster Care Eligibility, Adoption
Assistance, and Permanency Care
Assistance files, see 53011285:
Eligibility Case Files.

series_description:
Includes Temporary Substitute Care,
Family Reunification, Long Term Substitute

comments:
IMPACT codes CVS and PCR. For
eligibility records, including Foster
Care Eligibility, Adoption Assistance,
and Permanency Care Assistance
files, see 53011285: Eligibility Case
Files.

series_description:
Case record of placement with fictive kin
(relatives or close family friends).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 99 years
comments:
IMPACT code KHR. For eligibility
records, including Foster Care
Eligibility, Adoption Assistance, and
Permanency Care Assistance files,
see 53011285: Eligibility Case Files.

series_description:
Includes Foster Care Eligibility (FCE),
Adoption Assistance (AA), and Permanency
Care Assistance (PCA) eligibility files. This
series includes any unique eligibility
records, such as assistance agreement
forms and eligibility related
correspondence, that are not otherwise
included in the IMPACT record or in an
external case file for non-eligibility case
management purposes. Eligibility records
included in more than one eligibility file,
such as Foster Care Eligibility records
merged into an Adoption Assistance file,
are retained for the period required by all
merged files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Non-unique eligibility file records,
such as court orders and vital
records, that are used for non-
eligibility case management
purposes should be scheduled
according to the case type. Copies
of these records included in an
eligibility file are considered
convenience copies. Purge Note:
Eligibility records uploaded to the
electronic case management
system may be purged separately
from the case record at the period
assigned by this records series. For
financial records, see AIN
53009330: CPS Children's Funds
Management Accounting Records.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments: CPS Policy Handbook, 1463.3

IMPACT code CSP. For Reason to
Believe with allegation type Sexual
Abuse (SXAB), SEE AIN 53012037.

series_description:
Closed after complete investigation or
Family Preservation: Reason to Believe
(RTB) and Allegation Severity of Fatal (FTL).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 99 years
comments:
IMPACT code FTL. For Reason to
Believe with Allegation Type Sexual
Abuse (SXAB), SEE also AIN
53012035.

series_title:
Information and Referral
Calls and Non-Case
Related Special Requests
(NCRSR)
series_description:
Includes unknown case inquiries and non-
case related special requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 months
comments: IMPACT code IRC.

series_description:
Alternative response intervention during
intake or investigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: IMPACT code ARR.

series_title:
Intake or Investigation
Record with TLETS Check
series_description:
Intake or investigation record containing a
check in the Texas Law Enforcement
Telecommunications System (TLETS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
IMPACT codes CAT, CCT, CIT, CWT,
LAT, LRT, LTT, and LWT.

series_title:
Child Care Investigations -
Reason to Believe - All
Other Allegations
series_description:
Disposition is Reason to Believe (RTB) with
an allegation type other than Sexual Abuse
(SXAB).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years
comments:
IMPACT code LRB. For Reason to
Believe (RTB) with Allegation Type
Sexual Abuse (SXAB), SEE AIN
53012312.

series_title:
Child Care Investigations -
Reason to Believe - Sexual
Abuse
series_description:
Disposition is Reason to Believe (RTB) with
an allegation of Sexual Abuse (SXAB).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 99 years
comments:
For all other Reason to Believe, SEE
AIN 53012310.

series_title:
Child Care Investigations -
Admin Closed, Unable to
Determine, or Moved
series_description:
Disposition is Administratively Closed
(ADM), Unable to Determine (UTD), or
Moved (MOV).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
IMPACT codes LAD and LUT. For
case records with a TLETS check,
SEE AIN 53012080, Intake or
Investigation Record with TLETS
Check.

series_description: Disposition is Ruled Out (R/O).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 18 months
comments:
IMPACT code LRO. For case records
with a TLETS check, SEE AIN
53012080, Intake or Investigation
Record with TLETS Check.

series_description:
Intake Closed Without Assignment (CWA)
for investigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 18 months
comments:
IMPACT codes CWA and LWA. If the
record contains a TLETS check, SEE
AIN 53012080, Intake or
Investigation Record with TLETS
Check.

series_description: Calls, eReports, Mail, and Facsimiles.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
